aiExplanation.summary: The Runtime Engine is the execution pipeline that converts user inputs (keyboard, mouse, voice) into concrete OS actions through a chain of specialized handlers.
aiExplanation.beginner: When you press a keyboard shortcut, the system needs to figure out what you want and do it. The Runtime Engine is like a relay race — each step passes the baton to the next until the action is complete.
aiExplanation.technical: The pipeline follows a unidirectional flow: DOM keyboard events are captured by a global event listener, routed through a shortcut registry (Map<keybind, action>), resolved to Zustand store mutations, which trigger React reconciliation and Framer Motion mount animations. The entire flow executes synchronously within a single event loop tick, with animation scheduling deferred to requestAnimationFrame.
aiExplanation.recruiter: This demonstrates event-driven architecture design. Rather than hardcoding behaviors, the developer built a modular pipeline where keyboard inputs flow through a registry, resolve to actions, and trigger UI updates — similar to how real operating systems handle input events.
